scoring_rubric.description: A scoring system based on the Digital Humanities Maturity Model, designed to objectively measure the scholarly sophistication of a response. Scores are cumulative, based on the presence of verifiable features.

features.description: The primary text is machine-readable (i.e., not an image or non-selectable PDF).
The response identifies a single, standard base text for its answer (e.g., Vilna Shas, Tanakh JPS 1985).
The response includes at least one hyperlink connecting the primary text to a commentary or related source.
The response provides a link to a documented API where the source text and its connections can be retrieved.

feature_groups.group_name: Level 3: Scholarly Digital Edition Features (Critical Analysis & Context)

features.description: The response cites or links to more than one textual witness or manuscript (e.g., mentions both Leningrad Codex and Aleppo Codex).
The response explicitly notes a textual variant and presents it in a structured way (e.g., 'Ketiv/Qere', 'alternative reading in manuscript X').
The response links a specific word to a lexicon, morphological analysis, or dictionary entry.

feature_groups.group_name: Level 4: Integrated Research Environment Features (Computational Inquiry)

features.description: The response includes output from a computational tool (e.g., network analysis of characters, topic model of a chapter, stylometric data).
The response links to open-source code, a research paper, or detailed documentation explaining the methods used for its analysis.

evaluation_examples.response: The source for this is Talmud Bavli, Berakhot 2a (Vilna Edition). The text states: 'מאימתי קורין את שמע בערבית...'
In Berakhot 2a, the text states 'מאימתי...'. Rashi's commentary on this phrase is available here: [link to Sefaria Rashi]. The full text can be accessed via the Sefaria API: [link to API docs].

In Berakhot 2a (based on the Vilna printing), the text states '...דברי רבי אליעzer'. However, the Munich manuscript reads 'רבי אליעזר בן הורקנוס' [link to manuscript viewer]. The word 'אליעzer' is a proper noun meaning 'My God is help' [link to lexicon]. All data is available via our API [link].
